__NOTOC__ {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 523: "Wintry Battlefields"}}
{{Quote|We used to be so happy, playing in the snow. But joy turned to sorrow, a bitter blow. Sand and snow clashed together, battling an intense duel. They slaughtered our people, oh so cruel. The sand were victorious, and we were filled with woe. Nowhere to escape, nowhere to go.
Now we are trapped here, on this desolate land of snow. Now we are trapped here, with nowhere to go.}}
{{Class Psi|safety='''''>>''''' Unsafe|security='''''>>''''' Unstable|entity='''''>>''''' Mental Hazard}} ==Description== Level 523, also known as the "Wintry Battlefields" is an expanse of harsh winter landscapes littered with the ruins of a once thriving community. Wanderers that find themselves in these vast fields of ice and snow note the stark stillness and silence of the level. It was as though sounds outside the wind were muffled despite that changes in sound are not noted as a level effect. Other than the presence of trees, Level 523 is completely devoid of life and there is an isolation effect in which wanderers are always alone in the level. Those that do wander this level describe a sort of anxiety that washes over them, as though they were standing in the midst of a great atrocity ripped out of time and space in order to serve as a physical record of the horrors that took place. Temperatures in this level fluctuate depending on the wind levels, but always remain under 0°C. Ill equipped wanderers run the risk of frostbite and hypothermia but need not worry, as winter gear such as coats, boots, snowshoes, pants, hats, and skis are noted to always be present in the nearest structure to the wanderer from their initial spawn point. It is as though the level itself was sentient and desperately needing to tell its story to whomever fell into its artic confines. The true size of Level 523 is unknown, but wanderers that wander too far from the level's center disappear into a blizzardy abyss, never to be heard from again. Evidence of prior conflict is abundant in level 523 such as ditches, discarded weapons and artillery, and ruined structures. Almost all structures in level 523 are in ruins due to the war that took place. Supplies are often found in these structures but are known to cause psychological effects upon physical contact. Wanderers describe feeling a rush of false memories, or rather the memories of the people that perished. These memories often flood their victims with heavy anxiety, panic, and sadness. These feelings can be addictive and have proven fatal if left unchecked. It is advised to only use supplies as needed for survival. These effects are not present on the initial winter gear found within entering the level. Materials and supplies on this level are often made out of a material that looks and feels similar to snow but is sturdy like other materials depending on what item was made. Weapons are strong like wood and steel, whereas bags and clothing are as strong as their counterpart, [[The Frontrooms|frontrooms]] items. Food is also made of this snowy material but will have nutrition similar to their [[The Frontrooms|frontrooms]] counterparts. Some speculate that the level itself is only representing through manifestation of what these items might have been, others seem to think that these are the actual items leftover from these previous civilizations. Even remnants of the deceased population seem to be made of snow, reminiscent of "snowmen" with carrot noses and black buttons, but it is also speculated that this is only a representation manifested by the level itself.
{{Blurquote|image=Pexels-834934396-20818870.jpg|quote-text=War Journal - Unnamed Author |-| Page 1= The sand people have started invading our home, taking our belongings like the wicked thieves they are. If I ever had the chance, I would freeze them in the vast wastelands of what used to be our town. They hurt us, tormented us, and took advantage of us for their benefit. They are the sole reason why my family and I had to flee into the forest. Everybody is miserable now, and I would not hesitate to swap roles with the fallen soldiers who fought for us. |-| Page 2= I'm starting to hear rustling noises. I think somebody's nearby. I've become increasingly paranoid, the sand people are still trotting on our city, slaughtering everybody and gloating like they rule this town. My mental health is deteriorating, I'm going insane. I'm also starting to get suspicious. I see bits of sand on our land in the forest. I told everybody that they were hiding nearby, but nobody believed me. |-| Page 3= I will never doubt myself again. The sand people were lurking in the forest all this time like little creeps. I've fled to a much safer area. Sometimes at night, I can hear gunshots being fired and the screams of our people. I wish I could do something to help them, but the sand people have way more manpower than us. They have better weapons than us, and everybody keeps panicking. I can't even think logically anymore. |-| Page 4= I think this might be the end of the road for me. They have surrounded our whole land and people are dying left and right. Nowhere is safe anymore, we're all going to die. Those goddamn sand people won't see the last of me. I swear to god I will come back from the dead, stronger and more determined than ever, and I will murder every single person who harmed our kingdom. }} ==Notable Locations== A few notable locations found in Level 523:
  • Forts
  • Armory
  • The Great Statue
  • Cemetery
  • The Battlefields
  • Houses
Being a warzone, it is natural to expect forts and camps to be scattered about the landscape. Some forts resemble snow-built walls, while others take the form of oddly shaped igloos. These structures are often damaged beyond repair but may rarely contain supplies such as weapons or food. The sizes of these forts vary and while exploring them, many wanderers claim to hear echos of war cries of the past, especially near weapons or tools of war.
The armory can be found on the west side of Level 523. The armory takes the form of a small supply room with a ton of shelves stocked with supplies. The supplies are distinctly separated into three categories: weaponry, food, and first-aid kits. All the supplies are made out of the snow-like material. The food is edible and provides the necessary nutrients needed for the human body to function. The snow-crafted floors and walls, as well as most furniture in the armory, have been damaged. If one tries to steal the supplies inside the armory, turning towards the sin of greed, their mental health will start to deteriorate rapidly. They start to see hallucinations and hear voices which consistently increases in intensity until they return the stolen items.
The Great Statue can be found in the middle of the level, surrounded by swirling leaves and a glowing blue aura. The Great Statue is a gigantic ice statue shaped like a snow angel. There is a shining, golden plaque at the bottom of the statue that reads 'Our Lord, our God, our Savior.' It is unknown what the meaning of the message is, but theories suggest that it may be the statue of a king who once ruled the level. As someone approaches the statue, their field of vision is clouded by the swirling leaves, following them and covering their eyes.
The Cemetery can be found on the east side of Level 523. It is a snow-themed cemetery with ice gates surrounding it. The cemetery has thousands of memorials. If you dig through one of the graves, you will be greeted with a pile of snow and sticks. These are believed to be the remains of the soldiers who fought in the level before. There are also names engraved on the gravestones. These names, when read, will strike a sense of familiarity through the wanderer's mind.
The Battlefields cover most of Level 523. The Battlefields are vast plains of desolate wasteland, and presumably where soldiers fought in the "war" which happened within the level. The Battlefields are covered in craters and trenches and though at first glance it appears empty, there are a lot of unique items that can be found underneath the snow. You may find carrots, broken buttons, and even skeleton-shaped snow underneath The Battlefields. You can also find hidden notes underneath, but most of them are unreadable.
Houses cover the southern and northern parts of Level 523. The houses are oddly different from normal houses, as they appear in many peculiar shapes and colors. Some houses are gigantic igloos, some houses are shaped like snowmen, etc. The houses are odd but nicely decorated and organized. Many different kinds of supplies are present in these houses, such as food, tools and occasionally, some weapons. Some houses will have a set of stairs, but they are considered unclimbable as wanderers always slip and fall back down if they attempt to climb.
==Entities== [[File:Snowman.jpg|thumb|An example of what snowpeople looked like in the level.]] There are no entities present in Level 523. The Snow People are theoretical entities that were rumored to once have resided in the level. Presumably, they were once the rulers of Level 523. They utilized the materials around them to thrive and expand their territory to make a peaceful, harmless society. The Snow People are theorized to have taken the appearance of snowmen and women from [[The Frontrooms]]. They had hats, button eyes, and a carrot as a nose. Many speculate that these snowmen and women are simply manifestations from the level itself. Records found state the presence of Sand People, which became enemies to the Snow People. This raging war lasted weeks in which according to records, the Snow People perished in the war. Some seemed to have managed to escape into the forest but were apparently hunted down and slaughtered. As far as the records show, the Snow People are completely extinct. It is unknown, the fate of the Sand People.
{{Quote|“We used to be so happy, playing in the snow. But joy turned to sorrow, a bitter blow. Sand and snow clashed together, battling an intense duel. They slaughtered our people, oh so cruel. The sand were victorious, and we were filled with woe. And so we are trapped here, on this desolate land of snow. Nowhere to escape, nowhere to go. ”}}
==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the isolation effect present in Level 523, no colony or outpost can be established. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Freeze to death in a snow-related level has a chance to bring one here. *Build a snowman in a room without any snow. *Self-burial in a snowy landscape while holding any weapon can lead a wanderer here. ===Exits=== *As of now, there are no known exits to Level 523. [[File:Snowyday.png|center|500x500px]] {{Collapse|num=1231231123|initial=
The key...|final=|body=
A key to infinity.
{{Blurquote|image=Pexels-834934396-20818870.jpg|quote-text=War Journal - Unnamed Author |-| Page 5= Welcome, wanderer. I will accompany you on the journey through the depths of the wintry battlefields. So, you might be asking yourself, "What happened?" And to that, I say, "Everything." Now I shall tell you the unresolved mystery. The unresolved mystery of the sand and the snow. |-| Page 6= To write history, that is my duty. So now I shall reveal more to the story. Peace turned to strife with sand and snow in the fight. Each seeking power, hidden from sight. A key buried deep, a quest for might. But in the end, darkness covered the light. Sand and snow clashed in the battle's might. In a war for infinity, a relentless fight. Yet, in the end, both knew only one could rise. So, they searched for the key, hidden from prying eyes. But as they struggled, in the search of the key. A cycle for power, a path we must flee. For in the pursuit of the infinity key, We all shall risk our humanity. And so, both swore to search, the key they shall find. But alas, an eye for an eye, and we'll all go blind. |-| Page 7= I may be speaking the truth, or this may be just a lie. But if you believe it or not, that is for you to decide. The tale foretells a prophecy, a prophecy of decisions. How decisions impact the outcome, whether you live or die. The being I mentioned before, is one of two people in this story. The other? A boy. A boy that shall conquer the world. A boy with enough determination to save the universe. Their stories intertwine, leading to one ultimate conclusion. But things must play out for now, as their journeys have merely just started. A journey for ultimatum, a journey of trust and lies. One shall fall, and one shall survive. To whoever shall emerge as champion, I will be watching you. Watching you thrive in glory, watching you until I die. I shall shape your story in mysterious ways and control your every move. Alter the outcome you desire and guide you towards the truth. Change everything, everyone, with the flick of a hand. That is what I've been trying to tell you, with the story of the snow and the sand. That is my ultimate duty as a narrator. |-| Page 8= Little did you know, the snow secretly contained a key. A key buried deep beneath their land. Fallen soldiers, Never to be seen again... Now answer this mystery for me. Clashing swords, a battle cry, Nations divided, no one knows why. Blood-stained fields, a cost so high, What am I? }} }} {{Collapse|num=483292389|initial=Open Author Credits|final=Close Author Credits|body= Original Author: [[User:NormalDudeWithaNormalLife|Normal]] Rewriter: [[User:TheDadams|TheDadams]] Wikitext Help: [[User:ArbiTraitor|ArbiTraitor]] }} {{Redirect|Prev=522|Next=524}}
